ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indonesian martabak manis (sweet stuffed pancake). FOODON_03315091 (pancake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake with cheese and condensed milk filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full 10-piece portion (approximately 500g), representing one whole 24cm pandan cheese milk martabak manis shared between 2–4 people.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Manis (Sweet Stuffed Pancake) — nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient composition data for pancake batter (wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar), cheddar cheese, and condensed milk, combined with FAO/INFOODS SEA reference values for Indonesian sweet pancake analogues
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) and FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for pandan cheese milk martabak manis exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition: approximately 55% sweet pancake batter (flour, eggs, butter, sugar, yeast), 25% cheddar cheese filling, and 20% condensed milk drizzle. USDA SR Legacy entries used: Plain pancake (FDC ID 172263), Cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414), Sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 172187). FAO/INFOODS SEA values for martabak manis used as cross-reference anchor. Values are per 100g of the combined finished product.

gi_source.title: Pancakes made from packet mix — GI value 67 (glucose reference); analogous estimate applied to sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis) with cheese and condensed milk filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pandan cheese milk martabak manis. The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of 67 (medium) for pancakes made from packet mix (glucose scale). This is used as the closest analogous food. The high sugar and condensed milk content may elevate effective GI slightly; however, the cheese and fat content moderates postprandial glucose response. GI of 67 is retained as the best defensible estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (67 × 192.5g carbohydrate in 500g serving) / 100 = 129.0 GL units.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and saturated fat load
hypertension.guidance: Each 500g serving provides approximately 1,400mg sodium, which represents 61% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 1–2 pieces and avoid frequent consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content from condensed milk and sweet batter
type2_diabetes.guidance: A full 500g serving delivers approximately 192g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of 129, making this food unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion should be restricted to 1–2 pieces maximum and paired with a protein source to blunt postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from cheddar cheese and butter, combined with high sugar from condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A full serving provides approximately 41g of saturated fat, exceeding the American Heart Association daily limit of 13g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence limited to 1–2 pieces and should not consume it daily.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Cheese Milk Martabak Manis is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis) flavoured with pandan extract and filled generously with premium cheddar cheese, then drizzled with sweetened condensed milk. The dish is sold as a full 24cm round pancake cut into 10 pieces, weighing approximately 500g total. The batter is made from wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, yeast, and baking powder, producing a thick, spongy, slightly crispy-edged pancake with a soft, airy interior. The pandan flavour imparts a fragrant, grassy-sweet aroma characteristic of Southeast Asian desserts. The cheddar cheese filling melts into the warm batter, creating a savoury-sweet contrast, while the condensed milk adds sweetness and a creamy richness. Martabak manis is a popular Indonesian street food and restaurant dessert, widely sold across Indonesia and in Indonesian restaurants in Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, this dish is energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100g, with significant carbohydrate (38.5g/100g), fat (14g/100g, of which 8.2g is saturated), and moderate protein (8.5g/100g) from the cheese and eggs. The high sugar content (18g/100g) from condensed milk and batter sugar, combined with a medium glycemic index of 67, results in a very high glycemic load per full serving (GL ~129), making it clinically inappropriate for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian and typically halal. It is not suitable for those with gluten, dairy, or egg allergies.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for pandan cheese milk martabak manis exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition using USDA SR Legacy entries for plain pancake, cheddar cheese, and sweetened condensed milk, cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA martabak manis analogue values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using packet-mix pancake (GI 67) as the closest defensible reference. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted ingredient composition (approximately 55% sweet pancake batter, 25% cheddar cheese, 20% condensed milk) using USDA SR Legacy FDC IDs 172263, 173414, and 172187, cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA martabak manis analogue. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. 2. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Desserts' as martabak manis is primarily a dessert/snack food; dairy is a component ingredient rather than the defining food group. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (packet-mix pancake); no direct GI measurement for martabak manis pandan cheese was found in the approved GI database. 4. Glycemic load per serving = (67 × 192.5) / 100 = 129.0, calculated on the basis of 38.5g carbohydrate per 100g × 500g serving = 192.5g carbohydrate per full serving. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not directly measured for this specific product. 6. This dish is sold by Smile Martabak at Funan Mall, Singapore, via field survey. 7. The input food_name '(10pcs) Pandan Cheese Milk' has been canonicalised to 'Pandan Cheese Milk Pancake (10 pieces)' to remove menu-style formatting and clarify the food identity. 8. Indonesian local name is mandatory for Indonesian cuisine per G6 and has been provided. 9. No Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names are required for Indonesian cuisine per the multilingual matrix.
